refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation context (customer-defined target and period), enabling precise verification and design focus.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period using telemetry data and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms
Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery protocols
Link availability reporting and alerting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure managed by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Interaction with Safety Services for alerting on link failures
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management but requires well-defined interfaces with Vehicle (network hardware) and Teleoperation (control commands).

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover capabilities.
Risk of increased system complexity leading to integration challenges.
High safety criticality demands rigorous validation of failover mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring and alerting DPs in subsequent decomposition levels to address detection and reporting.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for teleoperation control and associated network link management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation commands and telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on link degradation or failure to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but less direct control over link redundancy.
Contains physical network hardware but limited in managing failover logic.
Primary module for teleoperation link management and failover control.
Receives alerts but does not manage link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within Teleoperation minimizes diffusion and aligns ownership with control responsibilities.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for hardware dependencies.
Risk of interface complexity with Safety Services for alerting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and modularize failover logic within Teleoperation to reduce coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear logical solution and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists due to hardware dependencies on Vehicle module.
Alerting interfaces with Safety Services require careful definition.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion, supporting ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and alert logs accessible through Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation is clear and aligns with system architecture.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FRs to address detection and reporting explicitly.
Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services.
Plan integration tests focusing on failover and alerting mechanisms.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and inclusion of monitoring and alerting DPs is needed to fully address the high safety criticality and verification completeness.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

revise.why: To explicitly capture detection, recovery, and reporting aspects for comprehensive coverage and verification.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Teleoperation module can implement failover logic effectively.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Vehicle hardware and Safety Services alerting.
Verification challenges for failover under real operational conditions.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability statistics.
Test results validating failover mechanisms and alerting functionality.
